name: Formaldehyde
note: Known human carcinogen. Banned as a cosmetic ingredient in the EU (Annex II); in the US it still appears in some nail and hair products.

name: Zinc pyrithione
note: Classic anti-dandruff active in many US shampoos. BANNED in EU cosmetics since 2022 after classification as toxic to reproduction; the EU market reformulated to alternatives.

name: Butylphenyl methylpropional
note: Floral fragrance BANNED in the EU since 2022 (classified toxic to reproduction). Still found in US perfumes, lotions and detergent-adjacent products.

name: Hydroxyisohexyl 3-cyclohexene carboxaldehyde
note: Fragrance BANNED in the EU (2021) as a potent skin sensitizer. Still permitted in the US.

name: Hydroquinone
note: Skin-lightening agent banned in EU cosmetics (allowed only in professional nail systems). The US FDA removed it from over-the-counter products in 2020; prescription only.

name: Dibutyl phthalate
note: Plasticizer once common in nail polish. Banned in the EU (Annex II, reproductive toxicity); still legal in US cosmetics.

name: Di(2-ethylhexyl) phthalate
note: Phthalate plasticizer banned in the EU as toxic to reproduction.

name: Coal tar
note: Banned as a cosmetic ingredient in the EU (carcinogen). US over-the-counter anti-dandruff and psoriasis products may still contain it.

name: Lead acetate
note: Former progressive hair-dye ingredient. Banned in the EU and, since 2018, in the US as well.

name: Isopropylparaben
note: One of five parabens BANNED in the EU since 2014 over endocrine concerns and missing safety data. Still legal in the US.

name: Isobutylparaben
note: One of five parabens BANNED in the EU since 2014 over endocrine concerns. Still legal in the US.

name: Phenylparaben
note: Paraben banned in the EU since 2014 (insufficient safety data).

name: Benzylparaben
note: Paraben banned in the EU since 2014 (insufficient safety data).

name: Pentylparaben
note: Paraben banned in the EU since 2014 (insufficient safety data).

name: Triclosan
note: Antibacterial restricted in the EU to a few product types at 0.3% max; endocrine and resistance concerns. The US FDA banned it from antiseptic washes in 2016 but it persists in some products.
maxPermittedLevel: 0.3% in limited product types (EU Annex V)

name: Methylisothiazolinone
note: Preservative and major contact allergen. The EU banned it from leave-on cosmetics and limits rinse-off products to 15 ppm; US use is less restricted.

name: Methylchloroisothiazolinone
note: Preservative allergen; EU permits it only in rinse-off products in a fixed mixture with MIT.

name: Benzophenone-3
note: Chemical UV filter with endocrine-activity findings; the EU cut the permitted level to 2.2% after an SCCS review. Banned for reef protection in Hawaii and elsewhere. Common in US sunscreens.

name: Cyclotetrasiloxane
note: Cyclic silicone classified in the EU as toxic to reproduction; restricted to trace levels in wash-off products.

name: p-Phenylenediamine
note: Oxidative hair-dye ingredient and one of the strongest known contact allergens; EU restricts concentration and requires warnings.

name: Quaternium-15
note: Formaldehyde-releasing preservative and significant allergen; being phased out by most manufacturers.

name: Resorcinol
note: Hair-dye ingredient with thyroid-effect findings in animal studies; EU restricts levels and uses.

name: Toluene
note: Nail-product solvent; neurotoxic at high exposure. EU restricts it to nail products at 25% max.

name: Homosalate
note: Chemical UV filter; the EU lowered the permitted level to 7.34% in 2022 after endocrine-activity questions. US sunscreens may use up to 15%.

name: Octocrylene
note: UV filter that can contain traces of benzophenone; EU limit 10%. Allergy reports are increasing.

name: Ethylhexyl methoxycinnamate
note: Chemical UV filter with endocrine-activity findings under EU re-evaluation; banned for reef protection in Hawaii.

name: Propylparaben
note: Paraben preservative; the EU capped it at 0.14% after an endocrine-focused review. Permitted more freely in the US.

name: Butylparaben
note: Paraben preservative with the strongest endocrine signals of the still-permitted parabens; EU caps it at 0.14%.

name: Cyclopentasiloxane
note: Cyclic silicone restricted in EU wash-off products for environmental persistence; personal-safety data is more reassuring than D4.

name: Selenium sulfide
note: Anti-dandruff active permitted with limits in the EU; possible carcinogen classification at high exposure.

name: Benzalkonium chloride
note: Antimicrobial and irritant; EU restricts rinse-off concentration.

name: Salicylic acid
note: Exfoliating acid; safe for most adults at permitted levels (EU max 2% rinse-off, 0.5% other), not for young children.

name: Retinol
note: Vitamin A anti-aging active. Effective, but the EU adopted limits (0.3% face, 0.05% body) in 2024 to control total vitamin A exposure.

name: Dihydroxyacetone
note: Self-tanning agent, EU-capped at 10%. Avoid inhaling spray-tan mist.

name: DMDM hydantoin
note: Preservative that works by slowly releasing formaldehyde; a common allergen and the subject of US class-action suits over hair loss claims.

name: Imidazolidinyl urea
note: Formaldehyde-releasing preservative; can trigger contact allergy.

name: Diazolidinyl urea
note: Formaldehyde-releasing preservative; can trigger contact allergy.

name: 2-Bromo-2-nitropropane-1,3-diol
note: Preservative that can release formaldehyde and form nitrosamines with certain other ingredients.

name: Sodium hydroxymethylglycinate
note: Formaldehyde-releasing preservative marketed as 'natural' (glycine-derived); same allergy considerations as the class.

name: Fragrance
note: An undisclosed mixture that can contain dozens of substances. The EU forces 26 named allergens to be listed separately; the US does not. The most common trigger of cosmetic skin reactions.

name: BHA (butylated hydroxyanisole)
note: Synthetic antioxidant; possible carcinogen classification from animal studies.

name: Cocamide DEA
note: Foam booster listed as possibly carcinogenic (IARC 2B) based on animal studies; California requires warnings.

name: Diethanolamine
note: Can form carcinogenic nitrosamines; the EU restricts free DEA content in products.

name: Triethanolamine
note: pH adjuster; nitrosamine-formation potential with certain preservatives, purity rules apply in the EU.

name: Benzophenone
note: UV stabilizer; possible carcinogen (IARC 2B). Related benzophenone UV filters carry their own limits.

name: Talc
note: Mineral powder; must be asbestos-free, and the EU warns against use on children's breathing zone. US litigation history is extensive.

name: Aluminum chlorohydrate
note: Antiperspirant active. SCCS reviewed absorption data and set safe-use levels; breast-cancer link remains unproven.

name: Polyethylene
note: Plastic used as scrub beads or film; the US banned rinse-off microbeads in 2015 and the EU's microplastics restriction phases it out of cosmetics.

name: Paraben (other)
note: A paraben preservative outside the well-studied methyl/ethyl pair. EU permits specific parabens at set limits and bans five outright.

name: Phthalate
note: Plasticizer family; several members are EU-banned reproductive toxicants. Often hides inside 'fragrance' in US products.

name: Petrolatum
note: Safe when fully refined; the EU requires proof of refining history because crude grades can carry contaminant hydrocarbons.

name: Mineral oil
note: Occlusive oil; same EU refining-purity requirement as petrolatum.

name: PEG compound
note: Polyethylene glycol family; safe in themselves, but manufacturing can leave traces of 1,4-dioxane, which good manufacturers strip out.

name: PPG compound
note: Polypropylene glycol family; similar profile to PEGs.

name: Sodium laureth sulfate
note: Workhorse cleansing surfactant; mild irritant, and 1,4-dioxane traces depend on manufacturing quality.

name: Sodium lauryl sulfate
note: Strong foaming surfactant; a known skin irritant at higher concentrations and contact times, not a carcinogen.

name: Ammonium lauryl sulfate
note: Cleansing surfactant similar to SLS; mild irritant.

name: Cocamidopropyl betaine
note: Mild coconut-derived surfactant; allergies trace back to manufacturing impurities rather than the molecule itself.

name: Phenoxyethanol
note: The most common modern preservative; EU-capped at 1% and well tolerated by most people.

name: Benzyl alcohol
note: Preservative and fragrance component; one of the EU's 26 labeled allergens.

name: Chlorphenesin
note: Preservative with an EU concentration cap; occasional irritation reports.

name: Sodium benzoate
note: Preservative; low concern on skin.

name: Potassium sorbate
note: Preservative; low concern on skin.

name: Disodium EDTA
note: Chelating agent that stabilizes formulas; poorly biodegradable but low human-health concern.

name: Propylene glycol
note: Humectant and solvent; occasional contact allergy, otherwise low concern.

name: Alcohol denat.
note: Denatured alcohol; can dry or irritate skin in high-alcohol formulas.

name: Isopropyl alcohol
note: Solvent; drying to skin at high levels.

name: Methylparaben
note: The best-studied paraben; SCCS considers it safe at permitted levels. Lower concern than propyl/butyl forms.

name: Ethylparaben
note: Considered safe by the SCCS at permitted levels.

name: Polyquaternium
note: Film-forming conditioners; low health concern, slow to biodegrade.

name: Behentrimonium chloride
note: Conditioning agent with EU concentration limits; irritant when overused.

name: Cetrimonium chloride
note: Conditioning/antistatic agent with EU concentration limits.

name: Dimethicone
note: Silicone smoothing agent; biologically inert, main criticisms are buildup and environmental persistence.

name: Silicone (siloxane)
note: Silicone family ingredient; generally inert on skin, environmental persistence varies by type.

name: Acrylates copolymer
note: Film-forming synthetic polymer; counted as a microplastic in some EU environmental assessments.

name: Butyl methoxydibenzoylmethane
note: The main UVA filter in US sunscreens; photo-unstable alone but low toxicity concern.

name: Ethylhexyl salicylate
note: Mild UVB filter; low concern at permitted levels.

name: Zinc oxide
note: Mineral UV filter; the benchmark for safe broad-spectrum sun protection.

name: Titanium dioxide (topical)
note: Mineral UV filter and pigment; safe on skin. The EU restricts inhalable forms, so concerns focus on loose powders and sprays.

name: Lanolin
note: Wool-derived emollient; effective but a recurring contact allergen.

name: Lactic acid
note: Gentle exfoliating AHA; increases sun sensitivity during use.

name: Glycolic acid
note: Exfoliating AHA; increases sun sensitivity, concentration matters.

name: Limonene
note: Citrus fragrance component; one of the EU's 26 labeled allergens (oxidized form sensitizes).

name: Linalool
note: Lavender-type fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen when oxidized.

name: Citronellol
note: Rose-type fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Geraniol
note: Floral fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Citral
note: Lemon fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Eugenol
note: Clove fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Coumarin
note: Sweet hay fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Cinnamal
note: Cinnamon fragrance component; among the stronger EU-labeled allergens.

name: Isoeugenol
note: Fragrance component; potent sensitizer with tight industry limits.

name: Benzyl salicylate
note: Floral fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Hexyl cinnamal
note: Jasmine-type fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Water
note: The most common cosmetic ingredient. No concern.

name: Glycerin
note: Humectant that draws moisture into skin. Safe and effective.

name: Aloe vera
note: Soothing plant extract. No concern.

name: Shea butter
note: Rich plant emollient. No concern.

name: Tocopherol (vitamin E)
note: Antioxidant vitamin. No concern.

name: Panthenol
note: Pro-vitamin B5, soothing humectant. No concern.

name: Niacinamide
note: Vitamin B3 derivative with strong evidence for skin-barrier benefits. No concern.

name: Hyaluronic acid
note: Moisture-binding molecule naturally present in skin. No concern.

name: Ceramides
note: Skin-identical barrier lipids. No concern.

name: Squalane
note: Stable, skin-identical emollient. No concern.

name: Jojoba oil
note: Plant wax-ester oil close to skin sebum. No concern.

name: Coconut oil
note: Plant oil; comedogenic for some faces but no safety concern.

name: Sunflower seed oil
note: Plant oil rich in linoleic acid. No concern.

name: Olive oil
note: Plant emollient. No concern.

name: Argan oil
note: Plant emollient. No concern.

name: Citric acid
note: pH adjuster. No concern.

name: Sodium chloride
note: Salt, used as a thickener. No concern.

name: Cetyl alcohol
note: Fatty alcohol emollient — unrelated to drying alcohols. No concern.

name: Stearic acid
note: Fatty acid emulsifier. No concern.

name: Glyceryl stearate
note: Gentle emulsifier from glycerin and fatty acid. No concern.

name: Caprylic/capric triglyceride
note: Light coconut-derived emollient. No concern.

name: Carbomer
note: Gel-forming polymer used in tiny amounts. No concern.

name: Xanthan gum
note: Natural thickener. No concern.

name: Allantoin
note: Soothing skin conditioner. No concern.

name: Bisabolol
note: Chamomile-derived soothing agent. No concern.

name: Green tea extract
note: Antioxidant plant extract. No concern.

name: Chamomile extract
note: Soothing plant extract. No concern.

name: Sodium cocoyl isethionate
note: Very mild coconut-derived cleanser. No concern.

name: Glucoside surfactant
note: Sugar-derived gentle cleansers. No concern.

name: Betaine
note: Sugar-beet-derived moisturizer. No concern.

name: Urea
note: Skin-identical humectant and keratolytic. No concern at cosmetic levels.

name: Kaolin
note: Clay used in masks. No concern.

name: Mica
note: Mineral shimmer pigment; health concern is low (sourcing ethics is the debated issue).

name: Iron oxides
note: Mineral pigments. No concern.

name: Beeswax
note: Natural wax. No concern.

name: Caffeine
note: Used in eye and scalp products. No concern topically.

name: PABA
note: Early UV filter banned in EU cosmetics over sensitization and photo-reaction concerns; largely abandoned in the US too.

name: Chloroacetamide
note: Preservative banned in EU cosmetics as a strong sensitizer.

name: Methyldibromo glutaronitrile
note: Preservative banned in EU cosmetics after an epidemic of contact allergy.

name: Benzoyl peroxide
note: Acne active treated as a medicine in the EU (not allowed in cosmetics); sold over the counter in US cosmetic-style products.

name: Musk xylene
note: Nitro-musk fragrance banned in the EU (persistent and bioaccumulative).

name: Musk ketone
note: Nitro-musk fragrance restricted in the EU; persistence concerns.

name: Methanol
note: Toxic alcohol tightly restricted in the EU (denaturant only); a hazard in some nail and cleaning-adjacent products.

name: Camphor
note: Fragrance/cooling agent; neurotoxic at high doses, restricted for children's products.

name: 4-Methylbenzylidene camphor
note: UV filter with endocrine findings; the SCCS recommended against continued use and the EU is removing it. Not FDA-approved in the US.

name: Iodopropynyl butylcarbamate
note: Iodine-based preservative; banned in EU lip and body-lotion uses, allowed at low levels elsewhere.

name: Climbazole
note: Anti-dandruff active with EU concentration limits.

name: Piroctone olamine
note: Modern anti-dandruff active; the common EU replacement for banned zinc pyrithione.

name: Kojic acid
note: Skin-brightening agent; the EU capped it at 1% after endocrine-activity review.

name: Arbutin
note: Plant-derived brightener that can release small amounts of hydroquinone; EU limits apply.

name: Ammonium thioglycolate
note: Perm and hair-removal active; strong irritant with EU concentration limits.

name: Sodium hydroxide
note: Hair-relaxer active; corrosive, with EU pH and concentration limits.

name: Toluene-2,5-diamine
note: Oxidative hair-dye ingredient and strong sensitizer; EU-restricted with mandatory warnings.

name: Ammonium persulfate
note: Hair-bleach booster; respiratory and skin sensitizer.

name: Lead compounds
note: Banned in EU cosmetics; a contamination concern in some imported kohl and sindoor products.

name: 2-Butoxyethanol
note: Glycol-ether solvent in some hair dyes and nail products; EU exposure limits apply.

name: Ethyl acetate
note: Nail-polish solvent; flammable and drying, low systemic concern.

name: Butyl acetate
note: Nail-polish solvent; similar profile to ethyl acetate.

name: Nitrocellulose
note: Nail-polish film former. Low concern.

name: Phenylbenzimidazole sulfonic acid
note: Water-soluble UVB filter; low concern at permitted levels.

name: Padimate O
note: PABA-derived UV filter; photo-reaction concerns, rare in modern EU formulas.

name: Bis-ethylhexyloxyphenol methoxyphenyl triazine
note: Modern broad-spectrum EU UV filter with an excellent safety profile — not yet approved by the US FDA, one case where EU sunscreens are ahead.

name: Methylene bis-benzotriazolyl tetramethylbutylphenol
note: Modern EU UV filter (nano form assessed by SCCS); not yet FDA-approved in the US.

name: Farnesol
note: Fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Benzyl benzoate
note: Fragrance fixative; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Benzyl cinnamate
note: Fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Alpha-isomethyl ionone
note: Violet-type fragrance; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Oakmoss extract
note: Classic perfume base and potent allergen; the EU limits its atranol content.

name: Treemoss extract
note: Perfume base allergen with EU limits.

name: Dimethiconol
note: Silicone conditioning agent; inert in use.

name: Phenyl trimethicone
note: Shine-enhancing silicone; inert in use.

name: Carbon black
note: Eyeliner/mascara pigment; the EU permits purified nano-assessed grades, while the US FDA long barred it before re-permitting one purified form.

name: Colloidal oatmeal
note: Soothing skin protectant. No concern.

name: Centella asiatica
note: Soothing botanical (cica). No concern.

name: Honey
note: Humectant. No concern.

name: Azelaic acid
note: Multi-tasking acid for redness and acne; well tolerated at cosmetic levels.

name: Bakuchiol
note: Plant-derived retinol alternative; good tolerance data so far.

name: Mandelic acid
note: Gentle exfoliating AHA; increases sun sensitivity during use.

name: Gluconolactone
note: Gentle PHA exfoliant and humectant. No concern.

name: Phytic acid
note: Plant-derived chelator and mild exfoliant. No concern.

name: Zinc PCA
note: Sebum-regulating zinc salt. No concern.
